# vite-vue-internationalization

> Vite plugin for typed Vue SFC translations using `<locale>` custom blocks, script-defined messages, Volar type injection, and optional locale-specific inline chunks.

This file is a compact guide for AI coding assistants working with projects that use `vite-vue-internationalization`.

## Package Entry Points

- `vite-vue-internationalization`: Vite plugin, message helpers, and runtime exports.
- `vite-vue-internationalization/runtime`: runtime-only APIs such as `createInternationalization`, `useLocale`, `useLocalizer`, `createComponentLocale`, and `createComponentLocalizer`.
- `vite-vue-internationalization/virtual`: TypeScript declarations for the generated virtual module.
- `vite-vue-internationalization/volar`: Vue Language Tools / Volar plugin.
- `virtual:vite-vue-internationalization`: Vite-generated app runtime module. Import this from app code, not from this package source.

## Correct `$locale` and `$l` Access

In Vue templates, use unwrapped values:

```vue
<template>
  <p>{{ $locale.env.appName }}</p>
  <p>{{ $locale.sfc.title }}</p>
  <p>{{ $l.sfc.count({ n }) }}</p>
</template>
```

In `<script setup>`, the injected bindings are computed refs:

```ts
const appName = $locale.value.env.appName;
const title = $locale.value.sfc.title;
const countText = $l.value.sfc.count({ n: 3 });
```

Do not blindly replace all `$locale.env.foo` occurrences with one shape. Template code and `<script setup>` code require different access forms.

## Transformation Scope

By default, the Vite plugin transforms Vue SFC files (`.vue`) that contain `<locale>` blocks or top-level `defineInternationalization()` calls.

When `sfcTransform: 'all'` is configured, the Vite plugin injects `$locale` and `$l` into every Vue SFC. This is intended for projects that need `$locale.env` or `$l.env` global dictionary access from SFCs that do not own local messages.

Plain `.ts` modules are not SFC transform targets. Do not assume `$locale` or `$l` is available in ordinary TypeScript modules. For non-SFC code, pass translated values in from Vue code or use an installed runtime instance through the public runtime APIs where appropriate.

Do not add empty `defineInternationalization({ 'ja-JP': {} })` calls to every SFC just to force VVI transformation. That is a workaround, not intended usage. It can inject unnecessary bindings and component options, broaden `inline-chunks` localization, duplicate more chunks than needed, and obscure which components actually own messages.

If an app needs global dictionary access in many SFCs that do not own local messages, prefer `sfcTransform: 'all'` over injecting empty dictionaries into user components.

## Message Sources

SFC-local messages can be defined in `<locale>` blocks:

```vue
<locale locale="ja-JP" lang="yaml">
title: タイトル
count: "{n} 件"
</locale>
```

Script-defined messages can be declared with `defineInternationalization()` at the top level of a normal `<script>` or `<script setup>` block:

```ts
import { defineInternationalization } from 'vite-vue-internationalization';

defineInternationalization({
  'ja-JP': {
    greeting: (values?: { name?: string }) => `こんにちは ${values?.name ?? '名無し'}`,
  },
});
```

When message functions are extracted or emitted into inline chunks, their source is serialized. Keep those functions self-contained and avoid closures over local variables.

## Build Strategies

- `virtual`: default strategy. Locale payloads are loaded through virtual modules and dynamic imports.
- `inline-chunks`: build-only strategy. Localizable chunks are duplicated per locale and static `$locale` / `$l` access is replaced with locale-specific output where possible.

In `inline-chunks`, the plugin still only starts from localizable Vue SFC modules. Ordinary `.ts` files are not rewritten as localization modules.

## Configuration

Configure the same plugin options in Vite and Vue Language Tools via `tsconfig.json`:

```json
{
  "vueCompilerOptions": {
    "plugins": [
      {
        "name": "vite-vue-internationalization/volar",
        "primaryLocale": "ja-JP",
        "buildStrategy": "virtual",
        "messageSyntax": "vue"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

`primaryLocale` is required, either directly in `vueInternationalization({ primaryLocale })` or through the matching `vueCompilerOptions.plugins` entry.

## Further Reading

- `README.md`: overview and minimal setup.
- `docs/en/getting-started.md`: application setup.
- `docs/en/messages.md`: `<locale>` blocks, global dictionaries, locale-only SFCs, and script-defined messages.
- `docs/en/build-strategy.md`: `virtual` vs `inline-chunks`.
- `docs/en/message-syntax.md`: placeholders, pluralization, linked messages, and ICU mode.
